tldr: Goa taxi union blocks Uber/Ola in most areas — use GoaMiles app (govt-backed, fixed fares).
Drug possession setups on beaches are not myths; NDPS Act means 1+ year jail, no bail.
Never give your passport as scooter-rental collateral — photograph every scratch before you ride.
North Goa for energy and nightlife; South Goa (Palolem, Agonda) for quiet and yoga.
Book accommodation 3+ months ahead for Dec 20 – Jan 10; prices triple without notice.

neighborhoods.name: Assagao
neighborhoods.vibe: Boutique cafés, female-founder stays, quiet lanes
neighborhoods.notes: The Project Café is here. Safer than Anjuna, walkable to Vagator. Best first base.

neighborhoods.name: Anjuna / Vagator
neighborhoods.vibe: Party beach, flea market, lively nights
neighborhoods.notes: Highest drug-offer and overcharge density. Great energy; go with a group after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Palolem, South Goa
neighborhoods.vibe: Backpacker beach, yoga, sunset shacks
neighborhoods.notes: Cleanest solo-female-friendly beach. Silent Noise Club on Saturdays is unmissable.

scams.title: Fake hotel/villa booking
scams.what: AI-generated photos, fake booking sites. You pay ₹20K–₹2L and arrive to nothing. Raised in Rajya Sabha 2025.
scams.avoid: Book only via Booking.com, MakeMyTrip, or direct hotel website. Call hotel to confirm before paying.

scams.title: Drug possession setup
scams.what: Stranger offers drugs; 'plainclothes cop' appears immediately demanding bribe. Minimum 1-year jail under NDPS Act.
scams.avoid: Zero tolerance. Walk away from all unsolicited beach offers. Never accept cigarettes from strangers.

scams.title: Scooter rental damage claim
scams.what: Pre-existing scratches 'discovered' on return; deposit or passport held hostage for 'repair' costs.
scams.avoid: Photograph every centimetre of the bike before riding. Never surrender your passport. Use Royal Brothers app.

scams.title: GoaMiles impersonation
scams.what: Unofficial drivers hold GoaMiles-style cards and quote fixed rates that are 2–3× the real app price.
scams.avoid: Only book through the actual GoaMiles app. Ignore anyone approaching you in the arrivals hall.

transport.tip: Govt-backed fixed-fare taxi. Book before landing. Only reliable pre-paid option.
App-based rental with GPS insurance. Saves ₹1,500+/day vs taxis.
Safe, AC inter-city routes. Panaji ↔ Margao ↔ Mapusa.

hiddenGems.name: The Project Café, Aldona
hiddenGems.why: All-women team, 130-year Portuguese mansion. Pool, art books, design-forward interiors. The insider solo-women pick.

hiddenGems.name: Sappadu, Assagao
hiddenGems.why: Women-run South Indian veg restaurant. Homely thalis, no tourist markup. Featured Tweak India 2025.

hiddenGems.name: Mapusa Friday Market
hiddenGems.why: Real Goan market without the Anjuna hawker scrum. Arrive 8am for fish section, leave before 11am heat.

preBookChecklist: Book accommodation via Booking.com and call hotel to confirm reservation
Download GoaMiles app before your flight lands
Save Goa Police helpline in phone: 0832-2423400
Buy travel insurance (World Nomads covers scooter accidents in India)
Check NGT/pollution odd-even days if visiting in peak season

do: Use only GoaMiles or pre-agreed fixed taxi fares — no street negotiations
Photograph scooter 360° before and after every rental
Carry a door-stop alarm for hostel/guesthouse rooms
Stick to South Goa beaches if it's your first solo India trip
dont: Hand over your passport as collateral for any rental or deposit
Accept anything from strangers on beaches — not even fruit
Trust anyone claiming to be an off-duty police officer demanding documentation
Book Goa villas or party packages through Instagram DMs

premiumPreview: Premium covers: the 3 hostels that actively vet solo female guests, full GoaMiles booking walkthrough with screenshots, a complete Palolem vs Agonda vs Patnem comparison, and the November shoulder-season hack that cuts mid-range accommodation costs by 40%.

tldr: Every auto near Hawa Mahal will try to take you to a commission shop — use Uber/Ola only.
Old City is best at dawn (7–9am) before heat and touts both hit peak intensity.
No real 'government gem emporium' exists at tourist spots — every one of them is commission-based.
Nahargarh Fort after 4pm is dramatically less crowded and the light is better.
Heritage havelis in Bani Park give you proximity to sights without the Old City chaos.

neighborhoods.name: Bani Park
neighborhoods.vibe: Residential, heritage hotels, quiet streets
neighborhoods.notes: 15 min from all major sights, none of the Old City noise. Most solo female travelers prefer staying here.

neighborhoods.name: Old City (Pink City)
neighborhoods.vibe: Heritage architecture, dense markets
neighborhoods.notes: Beautiful but exhausting solo. Go with a plan, use app taxis, leave before dusk.

neighborhoods.name: C-Scheme / MI Road
neighborhoods.vibe: Modern area, cafés, safe evenings
neighborhoods.notes: For evenings out — Tapri, Curious Life Coffee, Maa restaurant. Feels like any Indian metro.

scams.title: Gem/stone shop commission
scams.what: Auto/guide takes you to 'government emporium'; pressure sale of overpriced stones. Driver earns 30–50% cut.
scams.avoid: Book rides on Uber/Ola only. Any shop described as 'government approved' near a tourist site is not.

scams.title: Carpet / textile commission loop
scams.what: Multi-person tag-team sales with fake 'export price' discounts and manufactured urgency.
scams.avoid: Shop independently in Johri Bazaar or Bapu Bazaar. Leave any shop you were taken to by a driver.

scams.title: Fake entry tickets
scams.what: Touts sell 'skip-the-line' tickets outside the gate. Tickets are counterfeit; you pay twice.
scams.avoid: Buy tickets only at official ASI counters or on the ASI India website. No legitimate queue-skip exists.

scams.title: Elephant ride 'tip' demand
scams.what: Ride priced at ₹500; aggressive tip and animal feed demands post-ride with crowd pressure.
scams.avoid: Skip elephant rides entirely (ethical + overpriced). Amber Fort jeep taxi ₹200 is faster and cheaper.

transport.tip: Both work reliably in Jaipur. Always preferred over street autos.
Insist on meter. Hawa Mahal → City Palace benchmark: ₹40–60.
Best for Amber + Nahargarh + Jal Mahal loop. Ask hotel to arrange.

hiddenGems.name: Nahargarh Fort at sunset
hiddenGems.why: Best views of Jaipur's pink skyline. Rooftop café open till 9pm. Army presence makes it safe.

hiddenGems.name: Galta Ji Monkey Temple
hiddenGems.why: Ancient temple in a gorge — monkeys, pilgrims, zero commission touts. Best 7–9am.

hiddenGems.name: Rajasthali Government Emporium, MI Road
hiddenGems.why: Actual state craft store. Fixed prices, no commission, quality Blue Pottery and block prints.

preBookChecklist: Book heritage hotel in Bani Park or Civil Lines, not the Old City
Download Uber/Ola before arrival — don't negotiate with autos from monuments
Buy Amber Fort tickets on ASI India website to skip the queue
Check Jaipur Literature Festival dates if visiting in January
Carry ₹500–1,000 in small bills for bazaar shopping

do: Browse Johri Bazaar and Bapu Bazaar independently — no guide needed
Visit Amber Fort at 8am opening to beat both heat and crowds
Use Rajasthali on MI Road for authentic fixed-price Rajasthani crafts
Ask your hotel staff (not auto drivers) for restaurant recommendations
dont: Accept auto rides to 'government-approved' gem or carpet shops
Engage with any 'export wholesale' fabric pitch near tourist sites
Share your hotel name or onward plans with persistent strangers
Ride elephants at Amber Fort — choose jeep or walking instead

premiumPreview: Premium covers: exact Hawa Mahal early-access hack (saves 90 min queuing), two heritage hotels with women-only floors, 8-day Rajasthan circuit itinerary (Jaipur → Jodhpur → Udaipur), and female artisan workshop visits where the money goes directly to the maker.

tldr: Allow 3 full days minimum — Varanasi cannot be rushed and rushing makes you a better scam target.
Morning boat ride on the Ganges at sunrise is the single non-negotiable experience; book night before.
Never accept 'free' blessings, sindoor, or marigolds at ghats — a donation demand follows instantly.
Bhang lassi is legal here and widely served — know exactly what you're ordering before you drink.
Assi Ghat area is calmer and safer for solo women than the main Dashashwamedh Ghat strip.

neighborhoods.name: Assi Ghat
neighborhoods.vibe: Quieter south end, guesthouses, yoga, cafés
neighborhoods.notes: Best solo-female base. Morning yoga on the ghats, safe café culture. 20 min walk from main ghats.

neighborhoods.name: Godaulia / Dashashwamedh
neighborhoods.vibe: Dense, intense, highest foot traffic
neighborhoods.notes: Stay only if you want the full immersion. Overwhelming for first-timers. Touts are constant.

neighborhoods.name: Cantonment / Sigra
neighborhoods.vibe: Modern, hotels, away from ghat chaos
neighborhoods.notes: Budget mid-range hotels with working AC. Good if you want to retreat from the intensity.

scams.title: Ghat tout to silk shop
scams.what: Friendly 'student' or 'priest' offers to show you a lesser-known temple, ends at family silk shop.
scams.avoid: Decline all unsolicited guiding. Hire a licensed guide through your guesthouse only.

scams.title: Boat ride overcharge
scams.what: ₹200 sunrise ride quoted; ₹2,000 demanded on return claiming 'per person, not per boat'.
scams.avoid: Fix total price and occupancy in writing (phone note, photo) before boarding. Confirm: this is total price.

scams.title: Fake Ganga Aarti 'donation'
scams.what: Man with collection box claims proceeds go to the Aarti organisation. Not affiliated.
scams.avoid: Ganga Aarti is free to watch. No official donation collection exists. Ignore all collection boxes.

scams.title: Bhang-spiked food or drink
scams.what: Bhang (cannabis) added to food without disclosure. Disorientation leads to theft or worse.
scams.avoid: Only order from clearly labelled bhang shops. Never accept food/drinks from strangers near ghats.

transport.tip: Best for narrow gali (lane) navigation. Agree price before riding.
For Cantonment ↔ ghats. Insist on meter or fixed price.
Book at official pre-paid counter only — ignore touts outside.

hiddenGems.name: Manikarnika Ghat at dawn
hiddenGems.why: The burning ghat is visited by many but understood by few. Go at 6am with a licensed local guide who explains context respectfully.

hiddenGems.name: Blue Lassi Shop, Godaulia
hiddenGems.why: 75-year-old family-run lassi stall. The original. Cash only, arrive before 11am before the line forms.

hiddenGems.name: Ramnagar Fort
hiddenGems.why: Across the river, no touts, old royal collection. Takes half a day. Best combined with sunset river crossing.

preBookChecklist: Book sunrise boat ride through your guesthouse the evening before
Carry a scarf to cover shoulders and head at all temple and ghat sites
Download offline Varanasi map (Maps.me) — alleys have no signal
Confirm train onward to next destination — Varanasi Junction gets very crowded
Keep ₹100–200 in exact change for boat rides, rickshaws, and temple donations

do: Hire only licensed guides through your guesthouse, not ghat touts
Agree total boat ride price and passenger count before boarding
Stay in Assi Ghat area for calmer mornings and safer evenings
Attend Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat — arrive 30 min early for a standing spot
dont: Accept free marigolds, sindoor, or blessings from anyone at the ghats
Drink or eat anything offered by a stranger near any ghat
Follow anyone offering to show you a 'lesser-known temple' or 'secret ceremony'
Photograph burning ghats without explicit permission from the family present

premiumPreview: Premium covers: the 4 guesthouses with women-only floors on Assi Ghat, exact guide to navigating Manikarnika ethically, a Varanasi → Sarnath day-trip itinerary with female-led guide contact, and the Benares Hindu University campus walk (secretly one of the safest and most beautiful morning walks in the city).

tldr: Old Manali is for backpackers and café culture; New Manali has ATMs, pharmacies, and reliable transport.
Rohtang Pass requires an NGT online permit — book minimum 2 days in advance, slots fill fast.
July–August is peak monsoon; landslides close the Manali–Leh highway with zero warning.
Altitude sickness starts around 2,050m — acclimatise for a full day before trekking anywhere.
Solo after 10pm in New Manali: stay in. Old Manali lanes are fine until sunset, not after.

neighborhoods.name: Old Manali
neighborhoods.vibe: Backpacker cafés, Israeli vibe, chill lanes
neighborhoods.notes: Best solo-female atmosphere. Dragon Café, Café 1947, Dylan's Toasted & Roasted. Quiet by 10pm.

neighborhoods.name: New Manali (Mall Road)
neighborhoods.vibe: Busy, ATMs, transport, shops
neighborhoods.notes: Practical for logistics; avoid budget hotels here — they face the noisy bus stand.

neighborhoods.name: Vashisht village
neighborhoods.vibe: Hot springs, guesthouses, temple, calm
neighborhoods.notes: 15 min from Mall Road. Female-friendly guesthouses, sulphur hot springs, Vashisht Temple.

scams.title: Rohtang Pass fake permit service
scams.where: Near bus stands and travel agents
scams.what: Touts sell 'guaranteed permits' for ₹1,500–2,000. NGT permits are free and online only.
scams.avoid: Book only at https://rohtangpermits.nic.in — free, government website. No middleman needed.

scams.title: Solang Valley overcharge
scams.what: Snow activity prices quoted per-person but charged per-item post-activity with add-ons.
scams.avoid: Agree full itemised price in writing before any activity. Walk away if price changes.

scams.title: Fake trek guide
scams.what: Self-described guides with no certification take inexperienced trekkers on dangerous routes.
scams.avoid: Hire only ITBP-certified guides through registered trek agencies. Ask for licence number.

transport.tip: Overnight AC sleeper. Most reliable option; book on HRTC website.
For Solang Valley, Rohtang, Naggar. Pre-book via guesthouse.
Within New Manali and between Vashisht and Mall Road.

hiddenGems.name: Naggar Castle
hiddenGems.why: 15th-century stone castle with Roerich Art Gallery and valley views. Almost no tourists on weekdays.

hiddenGems.name: Café 1947, Old Manali
hiddenGems.why: The original Old Manali café. Israeli breakfast, good filter coffee, safe for solo mornings.

hiddenGems.name: Hamta Pass day trek base
hiddenGems.why: Hamta Pass (4,270m) trek starts near Jobra. Day-hike to Chika meadow is doable solo.

preBookChecklist: Book Rohtang Pass NGT permit at rohtangpermits.nic.in (free, 2 days ahead)
Carry altitude sickness tablets (Diamox) from a pharmacy before ascending
Download offline HRTC bus schedule — connectivity drops above Kullu
Pack a down jacket regardless of season — nights drop to 5°C even in July
Check Manali–Leh highway status on HP Traffic Police Twitter before Leh plans

do: Acclimatise for one full day in Manali before any high-altitude activity
Stay in Old Manali or Vashisht for the best solo-female guesthouse culture
Book treks only through registered agencies with ITBP-certified guides
Carry ₹5,000+ in cash — ATMs in Lahaul-Spiti area frequently run out
dont: Trek alone above 3,000m without an acclimatisation day and a registered guide
Ignore the 'road closed' signs on the Rohtang approach during monsoon
Pay any middleman for Rohtang permits — they are free and online-only
Drive rented bikes above Rohtang without proper gear and experience

premiumPreview: Premium covers: the 5 guesthouses in Old Manali rated safest by solo female travellers, exact Spiti Valley extension itinerary (Manali → Kaza → Chandratal, 7 days), monsoon-season alternatives when Rohtang is closed, and winter (Dec–Feb) Manali survival guide.

tldr: Rishikesh is the global yoga capital — but 30% of 'ashrams' are unregulated and target foreign women.
Laxman Jhula bridge is the beating heart of the backpacker belt; stay within 1km of it.
Bungee jumping and white-water rafting are legitimate and world-class here — use only registered operators.
Most of the town is alcohol and meat free; this is a genuine religious city, not a performance.
Monsoon (Jul–Sep): Ganges floods, rafting closed, ghats underwater — plan around this.

neighborhoods.name: Laxman Jhula
neighborhoods.vibe: Backpacker belt, cafés, yoga studios, shopping
neighborhoods.notes: Best solo-female base. Walkable to most activities. German Bakery, Little Buddha Café.

neighborhoods.name: Ram Jhula / Swarg Ashram
neighborhoods.vibe: Ashram-dense, religious, intense
neighborhoods.notes: Beautiful but requires constant polite boundary-setting. Better as day visit from Laxman Jhula.

neighborhoods.name: Tapovan
neighborhoods.vibe: Upscale yoga retreats, calm, riverside resorts
neighborhoods.notes: For mid-range to premium stays. Quieter than Laxman Jhula. 5-min auto ride.

scams.title: Predatory yoga ashram / guru
scams.where: Ram Jhula area and online listings
scams.what: Unregistered 'guru' targets foreign women for extended retreat. Isolation, manipulation, financial drain.
scams.avoid: Research on forums (TripAdvisor, Lonely Planet Thorntree) before booking any retreat. Never pay more than 2 weeks upfront.

scams.title: Rafting overcharge and fake safety
scams.what: Unlicensed operator quotes ₹400 'discount' rafting; no safety briefing, no life jacket inspection.
scams.avoid: Use only TURA (Uttarakhand Rafting Association) registered operators. Ask for licence number. Min age/weight rules exist for safety.

scams.title: 'Blessing' donation trap
scams.what: Priest offers forehead tilak or sacred thread; donation of ₹1,000–5,000 demanded before you can leave.
scams.avoid: Decline politely and firmly. No temple in Rishikesh charges for entry or mandates donations.

transport.tip: Cheapest and most used. Runs constantly.
Best for navigating the narrow lanes of the ashram belt.
UPSRTC or private Volvo. 6–7 hours, comfortable.

hiddenGems.name: Beatles Ashram (Chaurasi Kutia)
hiddenGems.why: Where the Beatles studied TM in 1968. Now an open ruin with excellent graffiti. Fee is worth it for the quiet.

hiddenGems.name: Neer Garh Waterfall
hiddenGems.why: Short jungle trek to a multi-tiered waterfall. Best on weekday mornings. Safe for solo.

hiddenGems.name: Chotiwala restaurant (original)
hiddenGems.why: The original 1958 restaurant has a rival next door with the same name. This one has the original costumed doorman on the left.

preBookChecklist: Research any yoga retreat or ashram on TripAdvisor and Lonely Planet Thorntree forums before booking
Book white-water rafting with a TURA-registered operator (ask for licence number)
Carry a scarf for temples and ashrams — exposed shoulders aren't welcome
Pack mosquito repellent — riverside location means evening bites
Check Ganges flood status if visiting June–September

do: Stay in Laxman Jhula area for the best balance of safety and social vibe
Book yoga classes at established studios (Sivananda, Parmarth Niketan) not street-tout pop-ups
Attend the Parmarth Ganga Aarti at sunset — free, spectacular, and genuinely moving
Use TURA-registered rafting operators even if they cost ₹200 more
dont: Pay more than 2 weeks upfront for any ashram or retreat programme
Accept invitations to private 'meditation sessions' with individual gurus
Raft with any operator who skips the safety briefing or life-jacket check
Swim in the Ganges during or after monsoon — current is dangerous

premiumPreview: Premium covers: vetted list of 6 yoga retreats safe for solo foreign women (with red flags to watch for), the Rishikesh–Chopta–Tungnath trek guide (3 days, beginner-friendly), best riverside guesthouses with women-only floors, and a Haridwar day-trip add-on guide.

tldr: Delhi Metro is your best friend — safe, AC, women-only coaches on every train.
Old Delhi (Chandni Chowk) is overwhelming and scam-dense; go with a plan and leave by 6pm.
Prepaid taxi booth inside IGI Airport arrivals is the only safe airport exit — ignore everyone else.
Connaught Place touts will claim the tourist office is closed/moved; it is not — walk in yourself.
South Delhi (Hauz Khas, Saket, Lodi Colony) is a different city — modern, safe, great food.

neighborhoods.name: Hauz Khas / South Delhi
neighborhoods.vibe: Cafés, boutiques, safe nights, metro access
neighborhoods.notes: Best base for solo women. Hauz Khas Village has rooftop bars; Lodi Colony has street art and Green's Café.

neighborhoods.name: Paharganj
neighborhoods.vibe: Budget hotel strip, backpacker chaos, near New Delhi station
neighborhoods.notes: Cheap but high harassment and scam density. Only stay if you're catching an early train and need proximity.

neighborhoods.name: Connaught Place / CP
neighborhoods.vibe: Commercial centre, heritage architecture, central
neighborhoods.notes: Good for day visits. Underground metro-connected. High tout density; walk confidently and ignore everyone.

scams.title: Tourist office 'closed/moved' scam
scams.what: Tout or friendly stranger claims India Tourism office is closed and offers to take you to a 'government-approved' travel agent.
scams.avoid: India Tourism Delhi office (88 Janpath) is open Mon–Fri 9am–6pm. Walk in yourself. Never follow strangers.

scams.title: Airport taxi tout
scams.what: Aggressive men claim your pre-booked cab cancelled and offer cash rides. Overcharge ₹2,000–5,000.
scams.avoid: Use only the official Prepaid Taxi booth inside arrivals, or book Uber/Ola before landing and walk to the app-taxi pick-up zone.

scams.title: Gem export investment scam
scams.what: Long con: 'businessman' offers you commission to carry gems as exports. Fake gems, real customs trouble.
scams.avoid: Never agree to carry anything for anyone. No legitimate gem exporter needs a tourist as a courier.

scams.title: Cycle rickshaw price switch
scams.what: Price agreed; demand doubles on arrival citing 'per person' or 'per bag' surcharges.
scams.avoid: State destination and total price before boarding. Confirm: 'This is the total, for both of us, for all bags.'

transport.tip: Safest and fastest. Women-only coach (first coach, marked pink) on every line.
Both fully operational in Delhi. Preferred for night journeys.
Official booth inside T2/T3 arrivals. Fixed fares, no negotiation.

hiddenGems.name: Lodi Garden at sunrise
hiddenGems.why: Mughal-era tombs inside a manicured park. At 7am: joggers, silence, photogenic mist. Zero touts.

hiddenGems.name: Hauz Khas Complex (village end)
hiddenGems.why: 14th-century madrasa and tomb above a deer park lake. End of Hauz Khas Village — walk through the boutiques to reach it.

hiddenGems.name: Paranthe Wali Gali, Chandni Chowk
hiddenGems.angle: The only tourist trap worth it
hiddenGems.why: 145-year-old lane of stuffed paratha shops. Genuinely delicious. Agree price before sitting down.

preBookChecklist: Download Delhi Metro app and top up a Metro Smart Card on arrival
Book airport transfer via Uber/Ola app before landing — prepaid taxi as backup
Save India Tourism Delhi helpline: 1800-111-363 (free, English)
Check air quality index (AQI Delhi) before travel — Nov–Jan can be severe
Book Agra day-trip train (Gatimaan Express or Shatabdi) on IRCTC app in advance

do: Use the metro women-only coach for every journey — it is safe and enforced
Walk into India Tourism office on Janpath yourself; ignore all claims it is closed
Explore Lodi Colony street art and Lodi Garden as a morning pair
Eat at Karim's in Old Delhi for lunch — old establishment, table service, safe
dont: Follow any stranger who claims to be a 'student' wanting to practice English
Accept auto or taxi rides from touts inside the airport arrivals hall
Walk Paharganj lanes alone after dark
Agree to carry any package or parcel for anyone, however friendly they seem

tldr: Taj Mahal at sunrise (gates open 6am) means 90 minutes of soft light before bus tours arrive.
Agra Fort and Fatehpur Sikri are world-class and almost always skipped — don't skip them.
Agra is a 2-night city maximum; most people do it as a Golden Triangle stop, not a base.
The area around Taj Mahal's south gate is the highest scam-density zone in India.
Book Taj Mahal tickets on the ASI India website the night before — gate tickets sell out.

neighborhoods.name: Taj Ganj (south gate area)
neighborhoods.vibe: Closest to Taj, maximum tout density
neighborhoods.notes: Budget hotels here but you'll run a gaunt of scams every time you step outside. Stay in Sadar Bazaar instead.

neighborhoods.name: Sadar Bazaar
neighborhoods.vibe: Lively market area, better hotels, 10 min to Taj
neighborhoods.notes: Best balance of convenience and calm. Dasaprakash for South Indian food; Pind Balluchi for North Indian.

scams.title: Fake Taj Mahal ticket office
scams.what: Official-looking 'ticket office' sells counterfeit tickets. You pay twice at the real gate.
scams.avoid: Buy only at official ASI booth at the gate, or online at asi.payumoney.com the night before.

scams.title: Marble inlay 'factory' commission
scams.what: Rickshaw takes you to 'government marble workshop'. Overpriced souvenirs with heavy sales pressure.
scams.avoid: Genuine marble inlay artisans sell in Sadar Bazaar at half the price with no commission markup.

scams.title: Auto-rickshaw price flip
scams.what: ₹100 agreed for return ride; ₹500 demanded on return citing 'waiting time'.
scams.avoid: Agree total return price including waiting before departing. Use Uber/Ola if available.

transport.tip: Mandatory for Taj Mahal approach — no petrol vehicles within 500m.
Available in Agra. Best for Agra Fort ↔ Taj ↔ Fatehpur Sikri day-loop.
Fastest train, 1h35m. Book on IRCTC 60 days ahead.

hiddenGems.name: Taj Mahal at sunrise, west gate
hiddenGems.why: West gate (Shilpgram entrance) has shorter queues than south gate. Book west-gate tickets.

hiddenGems.name: Mehtab Bagh at sunset
hiddenGems.why: Garden directly north of Taj across the river. Perfect Taj reflection in the water tank at sunset. Almost no crowds.

hiddenGems.name: Agra Fort inner zenana
hiddenGems.why: The women's quarters (zenana) inside Agra Fort have the best Taj view in existence — from Shah Jahan's prison cell.

preBookChecklist: Book Taj Mahal tickets online at asi.payumoney.com the evening before
Set alarm for 5:30am — Taj gates open 6am and sunrise light is 6:30–8am
Book Gatimaan Express or Shatabdi on IRCTC app well in advance
Carry exact change for e-rickshaw to Taj south gate (₹50–100)
Pack sunscreen and a hat — Agra has zero shade between sites

do: Enter via west gate for shorter queues, same monument
Visit Agra Fort the afternoon of arrival — it is genuinely spectacular and always uncrowded
Buy marble souvenirs from Sadar Bazaar artisans, not commission shops
Do Mehtab Bagh at sunset as your last activity — it is free of touts
dont: Buy Taj tickets from any booth on the approach road — official only
Accept free marigold garlands or 'blessings' outside the Taj gates
Agree to an auto return price that isn't fixed before you depart
Skip Fatehpur Sikri — it is 40km away and completely worth the detour

tldr: Mumbai local trains have women-only coaches — use them; the general coaches during peak hour are not safe.
Mumbai is India's safest major city for solo women at night, but marine drive after midnight is still solo-avoid.
Taxis (black-and-yellow) are metered and honest here; Uber/Ola also fully operational.
Colaba Causeway is pick-pocket central — use a crossbody bag with the clasp facing inward.
Dharavi is a thriving urban township, not a tragedy — the Reality Tours guided visit is genuinely excellent.

neighborhoods.name: Bandra West
neighborhoods.vibe: Trendy, safe, restaurants, nightlife, sea link views
neighborhoods.notes: Best solo-female base in Mumbai. Carter Road, Hill Road, Linking Road. Metro and local train access.

neighborhoods.name: Colaba
neighborhoods.vibe: Heritage, Taj hotel, cafés, tourist belt
neighborhoods.notes: Gateway of India, Leopold Café, Mondegar. Tourist-dense but safe. Pick-pocket aware at Causeway market.

neighborhoods.name: Juhu / Andheri West
neighborhoods.vibe: Airport-close, Bollywood adjacent, beach
neighborhoods.notes: Good for transit stays near airport. Juhu Beach at sunset is pleasant; skip after 9pm.

scams.title: Colaba Causeway pick-pockets
scams.what: Deliberate jostling in the narrow market lanes; wallet or phone lifted in the crowd.
scams.avoid: Crossbody bag, clasp facing your body. Phone in front pocket. Don't pull out wallet in the market.

scams.title: Taxi meter 'broken' scam
scams.what: Driver claims meter is broken and quotes fixed (inflated) fare.
scams.avoid: All Mumbai black-and-yellow taxis must use meters. If it's 'broken', take another cab or use Uber.

scams.title: Gateway of India boat 'tour'
scams.what: Unlicensed boat operators quote ₹200 for Elephanta ferry; takes you on an unscheduled 'tour' instead.
scams.avoid: Buy Elephanta Island ferry tickets only at the MTDC booth on the pier — orange, official signage.

transport.tip: First coach from Western Line; marked with a 'W'. Safe, fast, ₹5–20.
Fully operational. Better than autos for cross-city journeys.
Metered, reliable, iconic. Insist on meter — drivers almost always comply.

hiddenGems.name: Dhobi Ghat viewing platform
hiddenGems.why: The world's largest open-air laundry. Watch from the bridge on Dr E Moses Road. Zero touts, surprisingly meditative.

hiddenGems.name: Dharavi township tour (Reality Tours)
hiddenGems.why: 80% of profits go to Dharavi community projects. The best urban sociology experience in India. Book in advance.

hiddenGems.name: Worli Sea Face at sunset
hiddenGems.why: Less crowded than Marine Drive. Sea Link views. Locals jog here; safe, pleasant, real Mumbai.

preBookChecklist: Download Mumbai local train map offline — Western, Central, and Harbour lines
Book Elephanta Island ferry at MTDC pier booth, not from touts
Save Mumbai Police helpline: 100 and Women's helpline: 103
Check monsoon dates — July–August, Mumbai floods badly and local trains halt
Carry a small crossbody bag for Colaba Causeway shopping

do: Use the women's coach on all local train journeys — it is safe and air-conditioned on newer trains
Eat at Britannia & Co in Ballard Estate — 100-year-old Irani café, unmissable berry pulao
Walk Marine Drive at sunrise for the best crowd-free Gateway view
Take the Reality Tours Dharavi visit — context changes everything
dont: Travel in general coach of local trains during 8–10am or 5–8pm peak hours
Buy Elephanta ferry tickets from anyone other than the official MTDC booth
Leave valuables in your hotel room — use the safe or carry them
Dismiss Dharavi as unsafe — it is a working township and the tour is genuinely safe

tldr: Bangalore is India's most liveable city for solo women — the tech culture genuinely shifts the baseline.
Namma Metro (Purple + Green lines) is excellent; women-only coach in every train.
MG Road and Brigade Road are the tourist belts; Indiranagar and Koramangala are where locals actually go.
Traffic is brutal — build 2× the expected travel time into any schedule.
The microbrewery scene here is world-class and solo-female-comfortable; Arbor is the benchmark.

neighborhoods.name: Indiranagar
neighborhoods.vibe: Cafés, craft beer, boutiques, young crowd, metro-connected
neighborhoods.notes: The solo-female ideal base. 100 Feet Road for food and nightlife; safe auto culture.

neighborhoods.name: Koramangala
neighborhoods.vibe: Startup district, restaurants, 24-hour cafés
neighborhoods.notes: Great food at all price points. Social is the best microbrewery. Well-lit streets, Uber always available.

neighborhoods.name: MG Road / Brigade Road
neighborhoods.vibe: Commercial, hotel strip, tourist-adjacent
neighborhoods.notes: Fine for transit; hotels here are expensive and the neighbourhood is more chaotic than Indiranagar.

scams.title: Airport taxi overcharge
scams.what: Touts quote ₹1,500–2,500 for city-centre rides; KIAS pre-paid is ₹700–900.
scams.avoid: Use KIAS pre-paid taxi counter inside arrivals, or book Uber/Ola before landing.

scams.title: MG Road gem store
scams.what: Same gem commission chain as Jaipur but less common. Friendly stranger leads to 'export store'.
scams.avoid: Ignore unsolicited guided shopping from strangers. Shop independently in Chickpete wholesale market.

transport.tip: Purple and Green lines cover most tourist areas. Women-only coach, first in train.
All three fully operational. Rapido bike-taxi cheapest for short hops.
Covers routes metro doesn't. Route 500-series covers airport.

hiddenGems.name: Arbor Brewing Company, Indiranagar
hiddenGems.why: The craft beer that started the Bangalore microbrewery scene. Solo-female comfortable, excellent food.

hiddenGems.name: ISKCON Temple, Rajajinagar
hiddenGems.why: Massive, calm, beautifully maintained temple complex. Free entry, no touts, good prasadam meals.

hiddenGems.name: Cubbon Park morning walk
hiddenGems.why: 150-year-old park in the city centre. 8am: joggers, dogs, families. Safe for solo women at all daylight hours.

preBookChecklist: Download Namma Metro app and top up a Bangalore Metro smart card
Book hotel in Indiranagar or Koramangala, not MG Road corridor
Save Bangalore Women's Helpline: 1091 and Police: 100
Allow extra travel time — Bangalore traffic is genuinely bad at 9am and 6pm
Book Coorg or Chikmagalur day trip through a registered tour operator if extending trip

do: Base yourself in Indiranagar for the best solo-female food and café culture
Use Namma Metro for all metro-connected journeys — faster than road always
Visit ISKCON Temple at 7am for the morning aarti — peaceful and crowd-free
Eat at MTR (Mavalli Tiffin Room) in Lalbagh for the authentic Bangalore breakfast experience
dont: Accept cab rides from touts at KIA Airport — use the pre-paid counter or app
Plan more than 3 sights in a day — traffic will defeat you
Miss the microbrewery belt in Indiranagar/Koramangala for at least one evening
Ignore the day-trip options — Coorg, Chikmagalur, and Hampi are all reachable

tldr: Kolkata is one of India's safest cities for solo women — the street culture is genuinely different.
The Metro is clean, safe, and has women-only coaches; yellow taxis are metered and reliable.
Park Street is the nightlife and café spine; Ballygunge and Lake Road are the best residential bases.
Howrah Bridge at dawn (6–7am) is one of the most photogenic 20 minutes in India — and there are almost no touts.
Budget ₹3–4 days: Kumartuli potters' quarter and New Market are completely off most tourist itineraries.

neighborhoods.name: Ballygunge / Lake Road
neighborhoods.vibe: Residential, safe, cafés, easy Metro access
neighborhoods.notes: Best solo-female base. Quiet tree-lined streets, excellent food at all price points, feels like old Calcutta.

neighborhoods.name: Park Street
neighborhoods.vibe: Restaurants, cafés, colonial architecture, nightlife
neighborhoods.notes: Flurys for breakfast, Peter Cat for dinner. Well-lit evenings. Safe for solo dining and walking.

neighborhoods.name: Sudder Street (backpacker belt)
neighborhoods.vibe: Budget hotels, tourist-adjacent, central
neighborhoods.notes: Functional and cheap but scruffier than Ballygunge. Fine as a base; just less pleasant to walk around.

scams.title: Howrah station taxi overcharge
scams.what: Unofficial taxis cluster at exits; quote 3× meter rates to confused arrivals.
scams.avoid: Walk to the prepaid taxi booth inside both stations. Kolkata yellow taxis outside also use meters — insist.

scams.title: New Market 'closed today' redirect
scams.what: Tout claims New Market is closed for a holiday and offers to take you to a 'better' market (his commission shop).
scams.avoid: New Market is almost never closed. Walk past the tout and enter yourself.

scams.title: Fake donation for Mother Teresa's home
scams.what: Man with official-looking clipboard solicits 'donations' for Missionaries of Charity.
scams.avoid: Missionaries of Charity does not collect street donations. Donate only on their official website.

transport.tip: Blue Line covers Park Street to Howrah. Women-only coach, first in train.
Iconic and honest. Insist on meter — drivers comply.
Available; useful for areas not on Metro. App fares are fair.

hiddenGems.name: Kumartuli potters' quarter
hiddenGems.why: Where all of Kolkata's Durga Puja idols are made year-round. Clay sculptors working in tiny workshops. No touts, no entry fee.

hiddenGems.name: Flurys, Park Street
hiddenGems.why: Tea room open since 1927. Solo breakfast with pastries and filter coffee. Safe, civilised, excellent.

hiddenGems.name: Princep Ghat at sunset
hiddenGems.why: Victorian memorial arch on the Hooghly. Locals come for evening walks. Boats for hire. Almost no tourists.

preBookChecklist: Book hotel in Ballygunge or near Park Street, not Sudder Street if budget allows
Download Kolkata Metro app and buy a Metro smart card at any station
Check Durga Puja dates (October) — if visiting during festival, book months ahead
Carry ₹500 in small change for yellow taxi meters and street food
Save Kolkata Police helpline: 100 and Women's helpline: 1091

do: Eat at Kewpie's in Elgin for authentic Bengali home cooking — book ahead
Walk Howrah Bridge from the Kolkata side at 6am for zero crowds and best light
Visit Kumartuli on a weekday morning — sculptors are at work and welcoming
Use yellow Ambassador taxis — they are metered, honest, and a Kolkata experience
dont: Accept rides from anyone approaching you at Howrah or Sealdah station exits
Give money to anyone collecting for Missionaries of Charity on the street
Miss the Sunday morning book market on College Street — largest second-hand book market in the world
Rush Kolkata — it rewards slow travel more than almost any Indian city

tldr: Chennai has the best South Indian food in the world — if you leave without eating a proper tiffin breakfast, you have failed.
Marina Beach is the world's second-longest urban beach; it is safe at sunrise, avoid after sunset.
Auto-rickshaws here notoriously refuse meters — use Uber/Ola or negotiate firmly before boarding.
The MRTS and Chennai Metro together cover most tourist areas; women-only coach on Metro.
Mylapore is the cultural and religious heart of the city — older, calmer, and more authentic than T Nagar.

neighborhoods.name: Mylapore
neighborhoods.vibe: Temple culture, old Madras, filter coffee, silk sarees
neighborhoods.notes: Best solo-female base. Kapaleeshwarar Temple, Luz Church, cycle-rickshaw lanes. Morning filter coffee ritual.

neighborhoods.name: Nungambakkam / Egmore
neighborhoods.vibe: Hotels, Metro access, restaurants, central
neighborhoods.notes: Practical base with good mid-range hotels. Saravana Bhavan HQ is here — eat there.

neighborhoods.name: T Nagar
neighborhoods.vibe: Shopping, silk, crowds, congested
neighborhoods.notes: Great for silk and jewellery shopping but extremely crowded. Stay only if that's your primary purpose.

scams.title: Auto meter refusal
scams.what: Autos quote 2–3× Uber rates and refuse to use meters, especially from airport and Central Station.
scams.avoid: Use Uber or Ola exclusively in Chennai. If you must use auto, get Ola Auto — it's app-metered.

scams.title: Silk 'wholesale' store redirect
scams.what: Friendly local offers to show you the 'real' silk shop where 'only locals go'. Commission-based.
scams.avoid: Buy silk independently at Co-optex (government textile store, fixed prices) on Anna Salai.

scams.title: Marina Beach photography harassment
scams.what: Groups of young men photograph women without consent; camera pointed at face/body.
scams.avoid: Sunrise hours (6–8am) are peaceful. Avoid evenings. If photographed without consent, confront directly or walk to nearest police booth.

transport.tip: Green and Blue lines. Women-only coach. Covers airport to central.
Essential in Chennai — auto meters are unreliable. Use app-based always.
Covers coastal corridor from Beach to Velachery. Cheap and fast.

hiddenGems.name: Murugan Idli Shop, Mylapore
hiddenGems.why: Open since 1969. Idli, sambar, chutney. The standard against which all South Indian breakfast is measured. Queue moves fast.

hiddenGems.name: Kapaleeshwarar Temple at dawn
hiddenGems.why: The best Dravidian gopuram in Tamil Nadu accessible without a day trip. 6am: priests, flowers, no tourists.
hiddenGems.approxCost: Free for Hindus; non-Hindus welcome in outer courtyard

hiddenGems.name: Cholamandal Artists' Village
hiddenGems.why: Living artist community 20km south of Chennai. Galleries, studios, sculpture garden. Completely overlooked by tourists.

preBookChecklist: Book hotel in Mylapore or Nungambakkam — not T Nagar unless shopping is the focus
Download Uber/Ola before arrival — auto meter situation makes apps essential
Visit Murugan Idli Shop before 9am on your first morning
Check Pongal festival dates (January) if visiting in winter — major and beautiful
Carry a dupatta for temple visits — shoulders must be covered

do: Eat tiffin breakfast (idli/vada/dosa) at a proper Mylapore restaurant on day one
Visit Kapaleeshwarar Temple at dawn — the 6am rituals are stunning
Use Uber/Ola for all transport — saves the daily auto negotiation frustration
Go to Cholamandal Artists' Village if you have a half-day to spare
dont: Negotiate with autos outside Central Station or the airport — use app always
Visit Marina Beach alone after 7pm
Buy silk from any shop you were taken to by an auto driver or 'friendly local'
Skip the filter coffee — it is a defining Chennai experience and costs ₹20

tldr: Udaipur is Rajasthan's most romantic and solo-female-comfortable city — the pace is slower, the touts fewer.
Lake Pichola at sunset from a rooftop café is one of India's iconic experiences — and it is free.
Book heritage havelis in the old city for the view; book modern hotels in New City for the amenities.
The boat ride to Jag Mandir (not Lake Palace, which is hotel-only) is worth every rupee.
Holi in Udaipur (March) is internationally famous; solo women should visit with a group or organised event.

neighborhoods.name: Old City (Lake Pichola side)
neighborhoods.vibe: Heritage havelis, rooftop cafés, lake views
neighborhoods.notes: Best atmosphere. Walk to City Palace, Jagdish Temple. Rooftop restaurants with lake views are everywhere.

neighborhoods.name: Fateh Sagar Lake area
neighborhoods.vibe: Local Udaipur, less touristy, evening promenade
neighborhoods.notes: Where locals go in the evenings. Nehru Park island, ice cream stalls, quiet. Good mid-range hotels.

scams.title: Lake Palace boat ticket fraud
scams.what: Touts sell 'boat tickets to Lake Palace'. Lake Palace is a private Taj hotel — public boats go to Jag Mandir only.
scams.avoid: Book RTDC boat tickets at the official booth at Rameshwar Ghat only. Jag Mandir is the correct destination.

scams.title: Miniature painting 'artist' commission
scams.what: Artist invites you to watch him paint, then sells at inflated prices with social pressure to buy.
scams.avoid: Genuine miniature painting workshops exist (Shilpgram craft village). Browse independently; prices are fixed.

scams.title: Sunset rooftop 'cover charge'
scams.what: Minimum spend of ₹500–1,000 per person imposed only when bill arrives, not when seated.
scams.avoid: Ask explicitly about minimum spend before sitting. Most honest places display it on the menu.

transport.tip: Metered in Udaipur; drivers are generally more honest than Jaipur or Chennai.
Available and reliable. Best for airport and day trips to Haldighati.
Old City is compact and flat enough for cycling. Hire from most guesthouses.

hiddenGems.name: Bagore Ki Haveli museum
hiddenGems.angle: Best folk art collection in Rajasthan
hiddenGems.why: 18th-century haveli on the lake with 138 rooms of folk costumes, puppets, and daily cultural performances at 7pm.

hiddenGems.name: Shilpgram craft village
hiddenGems.why: Rural arts and crafts complex, 3km west of city. No commission touts. Miniature painting, block printing, pottery.

hiddenGems.name: Fateh Sagar at sunset
hiddenGems.why: The lake Udaipuris actually use. Boat rides, island park, kulfi stalls. Completely tourist-free.

preBookChecklist: Book lake-view haveli in Old City 2–3 months ahead for peak season (Oct–Mar)
Buy RTDC boat tickets at Rameshwar Ghat booth only — not from touts
Pack a light cardigan — Udaipur evenings are cool October through February
Check Holi dates if visiting March — book a structured event for solo travel
Carry ₹200 exact change for Jagdish Temple boat and Bagore Ki Haveli entry

do: Watch the sunset from Ambrai Ghat — free, no minimum spend, the best lake view
Visit Bagore Ki Haveli for the 7pm folk performance — it is genuinely excellent
Explore Shilpgram for honest fixed-price Rajasthani crafts
Hire a cycle for Old City — it is the best way to discover the lanes
dont: Buy boat tickets to 'Lake Palace' from touts — public boats go to Jag Mandir only
Sit down at a lake-view restaurant without confirming the minimum spend
Visit Monsoon Palace (Sajjangarh) without checking opening times — it closes early
Rush out of Udaipur — it is one of the rare Indian cities that rewards an extra day

tldr: Fort Kochi is one of India's most solo-female-comfortable neighbourhoods — slow, walkable, art-filled.
The Chinese fishing nets at dawn are the classic image but the light is only good 6:30–8am.
Kathakali and Kalaripayattu performances in Fort Kochi are tourist-facing but genuinely excellent.
Kerala backwaters day cruise (Alleppey/Alappuzha, 90km south) is the most-recommended solo add-on.
Monsoon season (June–August) is hot and very wet — but Kochi stays open and is half the price.

neighborhoods.name: Fort Kochi
neighborhoods.vibe: Colonial heritage, cafés, art galleries, Chinese nets
neighborhoods.notes: The entire solo-travel reason to come to Kochi. Princess Street is the spine. Walkable, safe, excellent food.

neighborhoods.name: Mattancherry
neighborhoods.vibe: Spice warehouses, Jewish quarter, antique shops
neighborhoods.notes: 20-min walk or ₹30 auto from Fort Kochi. Paradesi Synagogue, Dutch Palace, spice market. Very safe.

neighborhoods.name: Ernakulam (mainland)
neighborhoods.vibe: Commercial, transport hub, modern
neighborhoods.notes: Where the buses and trains are. Fine for transit; no reason to base yourself here if Fort Kochi has rooms.

scams.title: Kathakali 'donation' after performance
scams.what: Performers pass a collection box post-show and create social pressure for large amounts.
scams.avoid: Fixed-price ticket covers the performance. Tip generously if moved but ₹100–200 is appropriate — not ₹1,000.

scams.title: Spice shop overcharge, Mattancherry
scams.what: Shops near the synagogue quote tourist prices 5–10× the real wholesale rate.
scams.avoid: Shops 2–3 streets back from the main synagogue lane have honest prices. Compare at 3 shops before buying.

scams.title: Auto overcharge, Ernakulam
scams.what: Autos at transport hubs refuse meters and quote flat rates to Fort Kochi ferry.
scams.avoid: Take the government ferry from Ernakulam to Fort Kochi (₹5) — faster and infinitely more scenic than any auto.

transport.tip: ₹5 and a 15-minute scenic harbour crossing. Best transport in Kochi.
Short distances only. Agree price before; ₹50–100 most rides.
Fort Kochi is flat and compact. Most guesthouses rent bikes.

hiddenGems.name: Kashi Art Café, Fort Kochi
hiddenGems.why: Gallery and café since 1997. Solo breakfast with rotating contemporary art on the walls. The most civilised morning in Kerala.

hiddenGems.name: Kerala Folklore Museum, Ernakulam
hiddenGems.angle: Largest in Kerala, almost no tourists
hiddenGems.why: Three floors of masks, costumes, murals, and ritual objects. Extraordinary collection; entry is never crowded.

hiddenGems.name: Alleppey backwaters day cruise
hiddenGems.why: 90km south. Government DTPC boat (₹400, half-day) or private houseboat. The single best day trip in South India.

preBookChecklist: Book Fort Kochi guesthouse on Princess Street or Burgher Street 2+ months ahead in peak season
Check ferry timings from Ernakulam Boat Jetty — last ferry is around 9:30pm
Book Alleppey backwaters day cruise through DTPC Kochi or your guesthouse
Carry a light rain jacket October through May — Kerala gets unexpected showers
Save Kerala Tourist Police helpline: 1800-425-4747

do: Arrive at Chinese fishing nets by 6:30am for the best light and fewest people
Take the government ferry between Ernakulam and Fort Kochi — ₹5 and spectacular
Attend a Kathakali performance at Kerala Kathakali Centre (best in Fort Kochi)
Walk the full Mattancherry-to-Fort Kochi coastal path in the early morning
dont: Buy spices from the first shop near the Paradesi Synagogue — walk deeper into the lanes
Skip Alleppey if you have even one extra day — the backwaters are the whole point of Kerala
Miss the Kochi–Muziris Biennale if visiting in December–February — one of Asia's best art events
Take an auto where the government ferry goes — it is slower, more expensive, and less beautiful

tldr: Kasol is a tiny Parvati Valley village — the trek culture is real but the drug culture is equally real; know what you're walking into.
The Kheerganga hot spring trek (13km one way) is the single reason most people come — start at 6am.
Kasol village itself takes 2 hours to walk end to end; the charm is the river, the cafés, and the pine forest.
Israeli-influenced food scene is excellent — hummus and shakshuka at 2,000m are genuinely good.
Mobile signal disappears 2km past Kasol; download offline maps and tell someone your itinerary.

neighborhoods.name: Kasol main village
neighborhoods.vibe: Cafés, guesthouses, river views, trailhead
neighborhoods.notes: Everything is within a 10-minute walk. Stay on the main street for the best guesthouse options.

neighborhoods.name: Chalal (15-min walk)
neighborhoods.vibe: Quieter, riverside, local feel
neighborhoods.notes: Cross the bridge from Kasol and walk 15 minutes. Fewer tourists, better views, same trail access.

scams.title: Drug-laced food
scams.what: Cannabis added to food (brownies, lassi, tea) without clear labelling. Disorientation in remote terrain is dangerous.
scams.avoid: Order only clearly described items. If a café has a 'special menu' in whispers, leave. Ask explicitly: does this contain anything?

scams.title: Fake trek guide
scams.what: Unregistered guides offer Kheerganga treks; take groups off-route or abandon mid-trail.
scams.avoid: Hire through your guesthouse or registered trek agencies in Bhuntar. Ask for local ID and previous client contacts.

scams.title: Overpriced guesthouse at arrival
scams.what: Touts at the bus stop claim the 'best' guesthouses are full and take you to commission properties.
scams.avoid: Walk 5 minutes past the drop point independently. Almost no guesthouse is full except Diwali/Holi weekends.

transport.tip: Last bus around 6pm. From Bhuntar, which is on Delhi–Manali highway.
Faster than bus. Fills up at Bhuntar bus stand.
13km one way, 4–5 hours up. No vehicle access beyond Nakthan village.

hiddenGems.name: Kheerganga hot spring
hiddenGems.why: 13km trek through pine and rhododendron forest ends at sulphur hot springs at 2,960m. Camp overnight for the stars.

hiddenGems.name: Chalal riverside walk
hiddenGems.why: Cross the bridge, follow the river. Pine-scented, cold, quiet. Best at 7am before the café crowd wakes.

hiddenGems.name: Moon Dance Café, Kasol
hiddenGems.why: Israeli-run café with proper shakshuka, hummus, and filter coffee. Solar-powered. Seats 15. Opens 8am.

preBookChecklist: Download offline Parvati Valley map on Maps.me — no signal past Kasol
Tell your guesthouse your trek route and expected return time
Carry water purification tablets — no reliable clean water source on Kheerganga trail
Pack warm layers — temperature drops to 5°C at night even in July
Start Kheerganga trek no later than 7am to reach top before clouds close in

do: Start the Kheerganga trek at 6–7am for clear skies and cooler temperatures
Stay in Chalal for quieter, cheaper, and more scenic guesthouse experience
Tell someone (guesthouse, family) your exact trek plan before departing
Carry a portable charger — no charging points on the Kheerganga trail
dont: Eat anything described as 'special' without asking explicitly what's in it
Trek solo above Nakthan without a guide if it's your first Himalayan trek
Leave Kasol after 4pm for Kheerganga — you will not reach before dark
Assume the trail is marked — it is not consistently marked beyond Nakthan

tldr: Hampi is a UNESCO World Heritage Site spread across 4,100 hectares of boulder landscape — rent a bicycle.
The main bazaar side and Virupapuragadde (across the river) are two different worlds; most backpackers prefer the island side.
Sunrise at Matanga Hill (400 steps) is the single best thing to do in Hampi — set your alarm for 5:30am.
Hampi has no ATMs on the island side — withdraw cash in Hospet (13km) before arriving.
The boulder landscape at sunset turns amber-gold; the Royal Enclosure at 4pm is completely empty.

neighborhoods.name: Hampi Bazaar (temple side)
neighborhoods.vibe: Near Virupaksha Temple, guesthouses, restaurants
neighborhoods.notes: Best for temple access and sunrise on Matanga Hill. Mango Tree restaurant is here. Safe and walkable.

neighborhoods.name: Virupapuragadde (island side)
neighborhoods.vibe: Backpacker vibe, river views, sunsets, quieter
neighborhoods.notes: Cross the coracle (₹20). More relaxed, better sunsets, good cafés. No ATMs — carry all cash.

scams.title: Coracle price inflation
scams.where: River crossing between temple and island sides
scams.what: Official coracle fare is ₹20; touts charge ₹100–200 to first-timers.
scams.avoid: Fixed government rate is ₹20 per person. Join the queue at the official ghat and pay only ₹20.

scams.title: Bicycle rental damage claim
scams.what: Pre-existing damage cited on return; deposit held.
scams.avoid: Photograph the bicycle before riding. Rentals are cheap enough (₹100/day) to not need a deposit — decline shops that demand one.

scams.title: Guide 'royal tour' overcharge
scams.what: Self-appointed guides approach at major ruins and quote ₹2,000+ for basic walking tour.
scams.avoid: ASI-licensed guides are available through the ASI booth near Virupaksha Temple at fixed rates. Or use a good guidebook.

transport.tip: The only sensible way to see Hampi. Both sides have rentals.
Flat-bottomed boat, ₹20 fixed rate. Runs 6am–6pm approx.
For arriving/departing with luggage. 13km.

hiddenGems.name: Matanga Hill sunrise
hiddenGems.why: Best view in Hampi — the entire UNESCO landscape turns amber at dawn. Arrive by 6am. Safe, well-trodden path.

hiddenGems.name: Vittala Temple and Stone Chariot
hiddenGems.why: Musical pillars and the iconic stone chariot. Go at 8am opening — you'll have it almost to yourself before 10am bus tours arrive.

hiddenGems.name: Mango Tree restaurant
hiddenGems.why: Platforms over the river, banana leaf thalis, backpacker crowd. The Hampi ritual — lunch here after the morning ruins walk.

preBookChecklist: Withdraw all cash in Hospet before arriving — no ATMs on island side
Rent bicycle on arrival — it is the only way to cover the site properly
Set alarm for 5:30am on at least one morning for Matanga Hill sunrise
Carry 2L water minimum — Hampi in April–June is extremely hot
Check KSRTC bus or train times from Hospet to next destination before arriving

do: Cross to the island side (Virupapuragadde) for at least one sunset
Visit Vittala Temple at 8am opening before the group tours arrive at 10am
Photograph your bicycle before riding to avoid any damage dispute
Ask Devika's Hampi heritage walk recommendation at your guesthouse — local guides make the ruins come alive
dont: Pay more than ₹20 for the coracle river crossing — it is a fixed government rate
Arrive in Hampi without cash — the island side has zero ATMs
Visit the boulder landscape in the middle of the day in summer — heat is dangerous
Skip the lesser-visited Zenana Enclosure and Elephant Stables — they are empty of tourists and extraordinary
